# reply(toShouldUnselect:)

**Instance Method**

Notifies the main application of the preference pane’s ability to be deselected.

## Declaration

```swift
func reply(toShouldUnselect shouldUnselect: Bool)
```

## Discussion

If you override [doc://com.apple.preferencepanes/documentation/PreferencePanes/NSPreferencePane/shouldUnselect] to return [doc://com.apple.preferencepanes/documentation/PreferencePanes/NSPreferencePaneUnselectReply/unselectLater], you must invoke [doc://com.apple.preferencepanes/documentation/PreferencePanes/NSPreferencePane/reply(toShouldUnselect:)] when you have determined whether or not the preference pane can be deselected.

You should not override this method.

## Selecting and Deselecting the Preference Pane

- **willSelect()**: Notifies the preference pane that the main app is about to display the preference pane’s main view.
- **didSelect()**: Notifies the preference pane that the main app has just displayed the preference pane’s main view.
- **willUnselect()**: Notifies the preference pane that the main app is about to stop displaying the preference pane’s main view.
- **didUnselect()**: Notifies the preference pane that the main app has just stopped displaying the preference pane’s main view.
- **isSelected**: A Boolean value that indicates whether the preference pane is currently selected.
- **shouldUnselect**: A Boolean value that indicates whether the preference pane is able to be deselected.
- **NSPreferencePaneUnselectReply**: Constants that indicate the preference pane’s availability to be deselected.
